zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas Barber II於大約1524年出生在英國素福爾可福雷辛費爾德時，他的父親Sir James Barber39歲，母親Lady Alice Bohun35歲。他於大約1543年與Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor在英國素福爾可福雷辛費爾德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1599年6月28日在他的家鄉享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾韓吉 希頓聖 保羅 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Nicholas Barber II于大约1524年出生在英国素福爾可福雷辛費爾德时，他的父亲Sir James Barber39岁，母亲Lady Alice Bohun35岁。他于大约1543年与Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor在英国素福爾可福雷辛費爾德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1599年6月28日在他的家乡享年76岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾韓吉 希頓聖 保羅 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Barber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a barber, from Middle English barb(o)ur ‘barber’ (Anglo-Norman French barber, Old French barbier, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). In the Middle Ages barbers not only cut hair and shaved beards, but also practised surgery and pulled teeth.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name from German Barbier ‘barber’ (compare 1 above).
Catalan: occupational name for a barber, barber (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Berber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Salvador, Guillermo, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Luis, Adriana, Alfredo, Angelina, Armando, Carlos.
Catalan: probably a variant of   Barber  . This surname is most common in Mexico.
Turkish: occupational name from berber ‘barber’.

variants.name: Barbero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ricardo, Alvino, Ana, Angel, Arsenio, Fernando, Gustavo, Jaime, Jose, Maribel, Mario, Nenita.  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Domenic, Ernani, Giulio, Italo.  French Emile, Yves.

Spanish and Italian (Piedmont): occupational name for a barber-surgeon, barbero, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’ (from Latin barba); see also Barber . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Barbieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Rocco, Aldo, Cosmo, Dino, Domenic, Geno, Giovanna, Guido.
Italian: occupational name from barbiere ‘barber-surgeon’ (see Barber ), from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbour
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: occupational name from Old French barbeor ‘barber’. Compare the English form Barber .

History: George Barbour sailed from England on the Transport in 1635. He became a freeman of Dedham, MA, in 1647 and settled eventually at Medfield. A Scottish merchant named Barbour settled in eastern VA in the latter half of the 17th century.

variants.name: Borbely
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Antal, Ferenc, Ildiko, Kalman, Laszlo, Miklos.
Hungarian (Borbély): occupational name for a barber, Hungarian borbély. See also Barber .

variants.name: Barbeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Monique, Alcide, Alphonse, Amiee, Armand, Gisele, Jean Francois, Lucien, Lucienne, Michel.
French:

from barbeau ‘barbel’, a type of fish, hence a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, or a nickname for a man with a sparse beard, the fish being distinguished by beardlike growths on either side of its mouth.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

EARLIEST KNOWN BURIAL SITE: Elizabeth Nuchols Foxcroft Stansfield BIRTH	1544  Heptonstall, Metropolitan  Borough of Calderdale, West Yorkshire, England DEATH	5 Jun 1591 (aged 46–47) BURIAL     St Paul Churchyard, Hanging Heaton, Metropolitan Borough of Kirklees, West Yorkshire, England MEMORIAL ID	183424978

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas Barber II was born about 1524, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England, his father, Sir James Barber, was 39 and his mother, Lady Alice Bohun, was 35. He married Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor about 1543, in Fressingfield, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 28 June 1599, in his hometown, at the age of 76, and was buried in St Paul Churchyard, Hanging Heaton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Barber II(이)가 대략 1524년 영국 수폴크 프레징필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James Barber(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Bohun(은)는 35세였다. 그는 대략 1543년 영국 수폴크 프레징필드에서 Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1599년 6월 28일 그의 고향에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 핸긴그 히턴 스트 폴 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Nicholas Barber II родился около 1524 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir James Barber было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Bohun, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Grinling  Girling Barber  Barbor около 1543 г. в местности фрессингфиелд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 28 июня 1599 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности ст поул чурчярд, хангинг хитон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
